data.output:
1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"testing"
6	
7		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
8		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
9	)
10	
11	type backendConformanceCase struct {
12		name    string
13		backend string
14	}
15	
16	func backendConformanceCases() []backendConformanceCase {
17		return []backendConformanceCase{
18			{name: "jsonl", backend: BackendJSONL},
19			{name: "axon", backend: BackendAxon},
20		}
21	}
22	
23	// newBackendConformanceStore makes the backend choice explicit for the
24	// package-wide conformance matrix instead of relying on any default path.
25	func newBackendConformanceStore(t *testing.T, backend string) *Store {
26		t.Helper()
27		switch backend {
28		case BackendJSONL:
29			return newJSONLStore(t)
30		case BackendAxon:
31			return newAxonStore(t)
32		default:
33			t.Fatalf("unsupported backend %q", backend)
34			return nil
35		}
36	}
37	
38	func forEachBackendConformanceCase(t *testing.T, fn func(*testing.T, backendConformanceCase)) {
39		t.Helper()
40		for _, tc := range backendConformanceCases() {
41			tc := tc
42			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
43				t.Parallel()
44				fn(t, tc)
45			})
46		}
47	}
48	
49	func runBackendConformanceSuite(t *testing.T, tc backendConformanceCase) {
50		t.Helper()
51		makeStore := func(t *testing.T) *Store { return newBackendConformanceStore(t, tc.backend) }
52	
53		t.Run("create-get", func(t *testing.T) {
54			t.Parallel()
55			s := makeStore(t)
56	
57			b := &Bead{Title: "first", Description: "round-trip"}
58			require.NoError(t, s.Create(testCtx(), b))
59			require.NotEmpty(t, b.ID)
60	
61			got, err := s.Get(testCtx(), b.ID)
62			require.NoError(t, err)
63			assert.Equal(t, "first", got.Title)
64			assert.Equal(t, "round-trip", got.Description)
65			assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, got.Status)
66		})
67	
68		t.Run("update", func(t *testing.T) {
69			t.Parallel()
70			s := makeStore(t)
71	
72			b := &Bead{Title: "to-update"}
73			require.NoError(t, s.Create(testCtx(), b))
74	
75			require.NoError(t, s.Update(testCtx(), b.ID, func(bb *Bead) {
76				bb.Notes = "added by update"
77				bb.Priority = 3
78			}))
79	
80			got, err := s.Get(testCtx(), b.ID)
81			require.NoError(t, err)
82			assert.Equal(t, "added by update", got.Notes)
83			assert.Equal(t, 3, got.Priority)
84		})
85	
86		t.Run("claim", func(t *testing.T) {
87			t.Parallel()
88			s := makeStore(t)
89	
90			b := &Bead{Title: "claimable"}
91			require.NoError(t, s.Create(testCtx(), b))
92	
93			require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "alice"))
94			got, err := s.Get(testCtx(), b.ID)
95			require.NoError(t, err)
96			assert.Equal(t, StatusInProgress, got.Status)
97			assert.Equal(t, "alice", got.Owner)
98	
99			err = s.Claim(b.ID, "bob")
100			require.Error(t, err)
101	
102			require.NoError(t, s.Unclaim(b.ID))
103			got, err = s.Get(testCtx(), b.ID)
104			require.NoError(t, err)
105			assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, got.Status)
106			assert.Empty(t, got.Owner)
107		})
108	
109		t.Run("deps", func(t *testing.T) {
110			t.Parallel()
111			s := makeStore(t)
112	
113			root := &Bead{Title: "root"}
114			child := &Bead{Title: "child"}
115			require.NoError(t, s.Create(testCtx(), root))
116			require.NoError(t, s.Create(testCtx(), child))
117	
118			require.NoError(t, s.DepAdd(child.ID, root.ID))
119			got, err := s.Get(testCtx(), child.ID)
120			require.NoError(t, err)
121			require.Len(t, got.Dependencies, 1)
122			assert.Equal(t, root.ID, got.Dependencies[0].DependsOnID)
123	
124			tree, err := s.DepTree(root.ID)
125			require.NoError(t, err)
126			assert.Contains(t, tree, root.ID)
127	
128			require.NoError(t, s.DepRemove(child.ID, root.ID))
129			got, err = s.Get(testCtx(), child.ID)
130			require.NoError(t, err)
131			assert.Empty(t, got.Dependencies)
132		})
133	
134		t.Run("events-close", func(t *testing.T) {
135			t.Parallel()
136			s := makeStore(t)
137	
138			b := &Bead{Title: "with-events"}
139			require.NoError(t, s.Create(testCtx(), b))
140	
141			for i := 0; i < 3; i++ {
142				require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{
143					Kind:    "test",
144					Summary: fmt.Sprintf("event-%d", i),
145					Body:    fmt.Sprintf("body-%d", i),
146					Actor:   "tester",
147				}))
148			}
149	
150			events, err := s.Events(b.ID)
151			require.NoError(t, err)
152			require.Len(t, events, 3)
153			for i, e := range events {
154				assert.Equal(t, fmt.Sprintf("event-%d", i), e.Summary)
155			}
156	
157			require.NoError(t, s.Close(testCtx(), b.ID))
158			got, err := s.Get(testCtx(), b.ID)
159			require.NoError(t, err)
160			assert.Equal(t, StatusClosed, got.Status)
161			events, err = s.Events(b.ID)
162			require.NoError(t, err)
163			assert.Len(t, events, 3)
164		})
165	}
166	
167	func TestBackendConformance(t *testing.T) {
168		forEachBackendConformanceCase(t, func(t *testing.T, tc backendConformanceCase) {
169			runBackendConformanceSuite(t, tc)
170		})
171	}
172	
173	func TestBackendConformance_TransitionMatrix(t *testing.T) {
174		forEachBackendConformanceCase(t, func(t *testing.T, bcc backendConformanceCase) {
175			makeStore := func(t *testing.T) *Store { return newBackendConformanceStore(t, bcc.backend) }
176	
177			// Legal transitions: each sub-test creates a bead in the required starting
178			// state, applies the transition via SetLifecycleStatus, then asserts both
179			// Status persistence and any required Extra side effects.
180			t.Run("legal", func(t *testing.T) {
181				t.Parallel()
182	
183				type legalCase struct {
184					name       string
185					fromStatus string
186					toStatus   string
187					opts       LifecycleTransitionOptions
188					// wantExtra: non-nil value → stored value must equal it.
189					wantExtra map[string]any
190				}
191	
192				cases := []legalCase{
193					// proposed -> {open, cancelled}
194					{name: "proposed-to-open", fromStatus: StatusProposed, toStatus: StatusOpen},
195					{name: "proposed-to-cancelled", fromStatus: StatusProposed, toStatus: StatusCancelled},
196					// open -> {in_progress, blocked, proposed, cancelled}
197					{name: "open-to-in_progress", fromStatus: StatusOpen, toStatus: StatusInProgress},
198					{name: "open-to-blocked", fromStatus: StatusOpen, toStatus: StatusBlocked,
199						opts:      LifecycleTransitionOptions{ExternalBlockerReason: "external issue"},
200						wantExtra: map[string]any{ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason: "external issue"},
201					},
202					{name: "open-to-proposed", fromStatus: StatusOpen, toStatus: StatusProposed,
203						opts: LifecycleTransitionOptions{OperatorRequired: true},
204					},
205					{name: "open-to-cancelled", fromStatus: StatusOpen, toStatus: StatusCancelled},
206					// in_progress -> {open, closed, blocked, proposed}
207					{name: "in_progress-to-open", fromStatus: StatusInProgress, toStatus: StatusOpen},
208					{name: "in_progress-to-closed", fromStatus: StatusInProgress, toStatus: StatusClosed,
209						opts: LifecycleTransitionOptions{ManualClose: true},
210					},
211					{name: "in_progress-to-blocked", fromStatus: StatusInProgress, toStatus: StatusBlocked,
212						opts:      LifecycleTransitionOptions{ExternalBlockerReason: "upstream broken"},
213						wantExtra: map[string]any{ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason: "upstream broken"},
214					},
215					{name: "in_progress-to-proposed", fromStatus: StatusInProgress, toStatus: StatusProposed,
216						opts: LifecycleTransitionOptions{OperatorRequired: true},
217					},
218				}
219	
220				for _, lc := range cases {
221					lc := lc
222					t.Run(lc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
223						t.Parallel()
224						s := makeStore(t)
225						b := &Bead{Title: lc.name, Status: lc.fromStatus}
226						require.NoError(t, s.Create(testCtx(), b))
227	
228						require.NoError(t, s.SetLifecycleStatus(b.ID, lc.toStatus, lc.opts),
229							"legal transition %s -> %s must succeed", lc.fromStatus, lc.toStatus)
230	
231						got, err := s.Get(testCtx(), b.ID)
232						require.NoError(t, err)
233						assert.Equal(t, lc.toStatus, got.Status, "status must be persisted after transition")
234						for k, want := range lc.wantExtra {
235							require.NotNil(t, got.Extra, "Extra must not be nil when expected values are set")
236							assert.Equal(t, want, got.Extra[k], "Extra[%q] must match after transition to %s", k, lc.toStatus)
237						}
238					})
239				}
240			})
241	
242			// blocked -> {open, proposed, cancelled}: set up bead through open->blocked first so
243			// ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason is present, then verify it is cleared on exit.
244			t.Run("blocked-from", func(t *testing.T) {
245				t.Parallel()
246	
247				type blockedFromCase struct {
248					name     string
249					toStatus string
250					opts     LifecycleTransitionOptions
251				}
252	
253				cases := []blockedFromCase{
254					{name: "blocked-to-open", toStatus: StatusOpen},
255					{name: "blocked-to-proposed", toStatus: StatusProposed,
256						opts: LifecycleTransitionOptions{OperatorRequired: true},
257					},
258					{name: "blocked-to-cancelled", toStatus: StatusCancelled},
259				}
260	
261				for _, lc := range cases {
262					lc := lc
263					t.Run(lc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
264						t.Parallel()
265						s := makeStore(t)
266	
267						b := &Bead{Title: lc.name}
268						require.NoError(t, s.Create(testCtx(), b))
269						require.NoError(t, s.SetLifecycleStatus(b.ID, StatusBlocked, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
270							ExternalBlockerReason: "blocking reason",
271						}))
272	
273						pre, err := s.Get(testCtx(), b.ID)
274						require.NoError(t, err)
275						require.NotNil(t, pre.Extra)
276						require.Equal(t, "blocking reason", pre.Extra[ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason],
277							"ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason must be set before blocked-from transition")
278	
279						require.NoError(t, s.SetLifecycleStatus(b.ID, lc.toStatus, lc.opts),
280							"legal transition blocked -> %s must succeed", lc.toStatus)
281	
282						got, err := s.Get(testCtx(), b.ID)
283						require.NoError(t, err)
284						assert.Equal(t, lc.toStatus, got.Status, "status must be persisted")
285						// Extra may be nil after the last extra key is deleted — a nil map means
286						// the key is absent, satisfying the "cleared" requirement.
287						assert.Empty(t, got.Extra[ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason],
288							"ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason must be cleared after leaving blocked")
289					})
290				}
291			})
292	
293			// closed -> open via Store.Reopen: status changes and claim Extra is cleared.
294			t.Run("closed-to-open-reopen", func(t *testing.T) {
295				t.Parallel()
296				s := makeStore(t)
297	
298				b := &Bead{Title: "reopen-claim-cleanup"}
299				require.NoError(t, s.Create(testCtx(), b))
300				require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "worker"))
301				require.NoError(t, s.Update(testCtx(), b.ID, func(b *Bead) {
302					if b.Extra == nil {
303						b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
304					}
305					b.Extra["claimed-at"] = "legacy-claim"
306					b.Extra["claimed-pid"] = "12345"
307				}))
308				require.NoError(t, s.Close(testCtx(), b.ID))
309	
310				pre, err := s.Get(testCtx(), b.ID)
311				require.NoError(t, err)
312				require.Equal(t, StatusClosed, pre.Status)
313				require.NotNil(t, pre.Extra)
314				require.NotEmpty(t, pre.Extra["claimed-at"], "claimed-at must be present before reopen")
315	
316				require.NoError(t, s.Reopen(b.ID, "reopen for test", ""))
317	
318				got, err := s.Get(testCtx(), b.ID)
319				require.NoError(t, err)
320				assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, got.Status, "status must be open after Reopen")
321				assert.Empty(t, got.Owner, "owner must be cleared on Reopen")
322				require.NotNil(t, got.Extra)
323				assert.Empty(t, got.Extra["claimed-at"], "claimed-at must be cleared on Reopen")
324				assert.Empty(t, got.Extra["claimed-pid"], "claimed-pid must be cleared on Reopen")
325			})
326	
327			// Forbidden transitions: each must return a ValidateLifecycleTransition error
328			// and leave the persisted status unchanged.
329			t.Run("forbidden", func(t *testing.T) {
330				t.Parallel()
331	
332				type forbiddenCase struct {
333					name       string
334					fromStatus string
335					toStatus   string
336					opts       LifecycleTransitionOptions
337				}
338	
339				cases := []forbiddenCase{
340					// closed is terminal; every outgoing edge is rejected except ManualReopen.
341					{name: "closed-to-in_progress", fromStatus: StatusClosed, toStatus: StatusInProgress},
342					{name: "closed-to-blocked", fromStatus: StatusClosed, toStatus: StatusBlocked,
343						opts: LifecycleTransitionOptions{ExternalBlockerReason: "reason"},
344					},
345					{name: "closed-to-proposed", fromStatus: StatusClosed, toStatus: StatusProposed,
346						opts: LifecycleTransitionOptions{OperatorRequired: true},
347					},
348					// cancelled is terminal; every outgoing edge is rejected.
349					{name: "cancelled-to-open", fromStatus: StatusCancelled, toStatus: StatusOpen},
350					{name: "cancelled-to-in_progress", fromStatus: StatusCancelled, toStatus: StatusInProgress},
351					{name: "cancelled-to-blocked", fromStatus: StatusCancelled, toStatus: StatusBlocked,
352						opts: LifecycleTransitionOptions{ExternalBlockerReason: "reason"},
353					},
354					// in_progress -> cancelled is not in the transition matrix.
355					{name: "in_progress-to-cancelled", fromStatus: StatusInProgress, toStatus: StatusCancelled},
356				}
357	
358				for _, fc := range cases {
359					fc := fc
360					t.Run(fc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
361						t.Parallel()
362						s := makeStore(t)
363						b := &Bead{Title: fc.name, Status: fc.fromStatus}
364						require.NoError(t, s.Create(testCtx(), b))
365	
366						err := s.SetLifecycleStatus(b.ID, fc.toStatus, fc.opts)
367						require.Error(t, err, "forbidden transition %s -> %s must return an error", fc.fromStatus, fc.toStatus)
368	
369						got, getErr := s.Get(testCtx(), b.ID)
370						require.NoError(t, getErr)
371						assert.Equal(t, fc.fromStatus, got.Status,
372							"status must not change after rejected transition")
373					})
374				}
375			})
376		})
377	}
378
